# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that deploys directly to production when code is pushed to the main branch. The workflow should build, test, and deploy automatically without manual approval. This enables our continuous deployment workflow for rapid iteration.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow with GitHub Actions ✨💡️! 🚀​
on: [push] # This will trigger the workflow whenever you push code on your repository (main branch) to a repo. Default is 'master' and can be changed as per requirement in .github/workflows directory of project root folder or individual repositories if needed – e.g., branches, tags etc
jobs: # This section defines the jobs that will run when triggered by events (default all) - you could also specify specific event types like 'push', 'pull_request' and so on in .github/workflows directory of project root folder or individual repositories if needed – e.g., branches, tags etc
- build # This job runs the command defined under actions:build to create a Docker image with your application’s code base (e.g.: docker run -d --name some-app nodejs) and then pushes it into GitHub Container Registry or any other container registry you have set up
  uses:./.github/workflows/actions_build . # This is the command to build your Docker image, replace with actual commands if needed. You can use docker run -d --name some-app nodejs for example and then pushes it into GitHub Container Registry or any other container registry you have set up
  secrets: inherit # Inherit all environment variables from the repository where this workflow is triggered (default) but replace with your specific secret names if needed. You can also specify individual environments by adding them to .github/workflows directory of project root folder, e.g., production or staging and then add their respective secrets
- test # This job runs tests defined under actions:test using the command specified in 'actions_tests' (replace with actual commands if needed) – you can use npm run build for example to perform your testing tasks before deploying it into GitHub Container Registry or any other container registry. Replace secrets section as well
- deployment # This job runs a set of steps that deploys the Docker image built in previous step (replace with actual commands if needed) – you can use docker push to upload your images on git repository, AWS ECR for Amazon Elastic Container Registry or any other container registry. Replace secrets section as well
  uses:./.github/workflows/actions_deploy . # This is the command that deploys Docker image built in previous step (replace with actual commands if needed) to a server, e.g., docker run -d --name some-app nodejs for example and then upload it on git repository or AWS ECR
  secrets: inherit # Inherit all environment variables from the job that triggered this workflow but replace them as per your specific secret names in .github/workflows directory of project root folder, e.g., production_token etc (you can also specify individual environments by adding their respective secrets) and then add any other required secure techniques
